Loupe view: fullscreen toggle via double-click, Enter, shortcut, Esc to exit
description:
Loupe (single image viewer) needs fullscreen support:

- Double-click thumbnail in Grid → opens Loupe fullscreen
- Enter key on focused thumbnail → opens Loupe fullscreen  
- Keyboard shortcut (e.g. F or ⌘F) → toggles fullscreen from any view
- Escape → exits fullscreen / returns to Grid
- Full window size by default when entering Loupe
- True macOS fullscreen (green button) also supported

Loupe already specified in design doc (view mode #3) with: pixel-level zoom, metadata panel, region selection, detection overlays. This issue adds the fullscreen entry/exit interactions.
close_reason: Implemented: F key and double-click toggle fullscreen in Loupe

title: DB corruption causes crash with no recovery UI
URL protocol scheme for agent-driven launch with precise parameters
description:
Agents must be able to launch ImageView via URL protocol with precise parameters.

## URL Protocol Examples

imageview://open?path=/path/to/image.png&zoom=200&view=loupe
imageview://open?folder=/path/to/folder&view=grid&size=160
imageview://compare?ids=img-1,img-2,img-3
imageview://open?paths=/a.png,/b.png,/c.png&view=grid&focus=1
imageview://open?folder=/path&filter=min_size:256&sort=rating

## Parameters
- path: single image path
- paths: comma-separated image paths
- folder: folder to import/open
- view: grid|compare|loupe|canvas|lineage|embeddings
- zoom: zoom percentage (100=fit, 200=2x, etc.)
- size: thumbnail size in grid view (px)
- focus: index of image to focus (0-based)
- filter: query string for filtering (min_size, rating, format, etc.)
- sort: sort order (date, name, rating, size)
- position: window position x,y
- fullscreen: true/false

## Implementation
1. Register imageview:// URL scheme in tauri.conf.json (deep linking plugin)
2. Parse URL parameters in Rust setup handler
3. Apply parameters to app state on launch
4. If app already running, handle as IPC message (single instance)
5. CLI should also accept same parameters: imageview open --path /a.png --zoom 200

## Also needed for MCP
The MCP server should be able to trigger these same actions programmatically.

title: Thumbnails have no accessible names - role=button with no label
Collections system — custom ordered sets with export to folders/formats
description:
Collections system — custom ordered sets with export to folders/formats.

## Creation Methods (all from Grid view)
1. **C key** — create collection from currently selected images
2. **Shift+C** — create from inverse (everything NOT selected / not rejected)
3. **B key** — enter Collect mode, Space adds images one by one in order, B again to finish
4. **Drag** — drag selected images to sidebar COLLECTIONS area to create/add to collection
5. **Templates** — create collection from a filter preset (e.g. 'all ★4+', 'all accepted', 'all PNG > 1024px')

## Smart Scenarios
- Select 5 in Grid → C → name → instant collection of 5
- Reject 3 with X → Shift+C → collection of everything else
- Filter ★≥4 → Cmd+A → C → collection of all high-rated
- Press B → browse and Space to pick favorites → B to finish
- Drag selection to 'New Collection' drop zone in sidebar
- Apply template 'accepted PNG > 512px' → auto-generates collection

## UI
- Collections in sidebar under PROJECTS/COLLECTIONS
- Within collection: reorderable grid with index numbers
- Drag-and-drop + Ctrl+Up/Down for keyboard reorder
- Grid view stays read-only

## Export
- Presets: web-ready, print, thumbnail-set, custom
- Template naming: {collection}/{index}_{name}.{format}
- Format conversion: PNG/JPEG/WebP with quality control
- Optional resize on export

Filter images by minimum size (width/height) - brainstorm UI for filter controls in sidebar
description:
Filter images by minimum size (width/height). Brainstorm UI first.

## Interface Options

A) Sidebar filter section -- Under FILTERS section, add min-size input like 'min: 256px' with text field or slider. Simple, always visible.

B) Status bar filter chip -- Click filter icon in status bar for dropdown: 'Width >= ___ Height >= ___'. Compact, out of the way.

C) Keyboard-triggered -- Press 'f' for filter mode, type 'size>256' in command palette-style input. Most terminal-native.

D) Sidebar slider with presets -- Slider with presets: All, >64px, >256px, >512px, >1024px. One click to filter.

E) Combine D + C -- Sidebar slider for quick access, keyboard shortcut for power users.
